definition: The equity risk premium at 2.5-3.0% (cap-weighted) is inconsistent with the positive stock-bond correlation regime that term premium rebuilding is producing. Sub-3% ERP was justified by negative stock-bond correlation, low inflation, and a central bank put—all three have deteriorated. Fair P/E may need to drop from ~21x to ~17-18x if ERP re-rates to 3.5-4.0%.
evidence: Gordon Growth Model sensitivity implies fair P/E drops from ~21x to ~17-18x on 50-100bp ERP re-rating
200bp cap-weighted vs equal-weighted ERP gap is the largest since 1999-2000
All three justifications for sub-3% ERP have deteriorated
open_questions: Is the positive stock-bond correlation regime permanent or could it revert if inflation returns below 2%?
Could AI productivity gains justify structurally lower ERP despite regime change?
How long can markets sustain 'inconsistent' pricing before correction?
